zh.Oxford, Oxfordshire, England: 英國英格蘭奧可福爾奧可福德
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke於大約1121年出生在英國奧可福爾。她於大約1148年與Sir Edmund Brooke在英國英格蘭奧可福爾奧可福德結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Oxford, Oxfordshire, England: 英国英格兰奧可福爾奧可福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke于大约1121年出生在英国奧可福爾。她于大约1148年与Sir Edmund Brooke在英国英格兰奧可福爾奧可福德结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Brooke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Brook  , which preserves a trace of the Old English dative singular case, originally used after a preposition (e.g. ‘at the brook’).

History: In 1650, Robert and Mary Mainwaring Brooke brought ten children and a number of servants with them from England to MD, where Robert became governor. Although the fourteen known contemporary Brooke immigrants in VA included Robert's brothers Richard and Humphrey, the relationships of the others are unknown. Brooke family memorials remain in the Anglican church at Whitchurch, Hampshire, England.

variants.name: Brook
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a brook, stream, water-meadows or low, marshy ground, from Middle English brook, Old English brōc ‘brook, stream’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, such as Brook (Kent, Rutland) or Brooke (Norfolk).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a water meadow or marsh, from Low German brook (cognate with German Bruch ‘swamp’ and Old English brōc; see 1 above).
Americanized form of Dutch Broek: topographic name from broek, a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke was born about 1121, in Oxfordshire, England. She married Sir Edmund Brooke about 1148, in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke est née environ 1121, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir Edmund Brooke environ 1148, à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke wurde um 1121 in Oxfordshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1148 in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Edmund Brooke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke è nata il circa 1121 nel Oxfordshire, England. Lei sposato Sir Edmund Brooke circa 1148, nel Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Oxford, Oxfordshire, England: イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアオクスフォード
ja.Oxfordshire, England: イギリスオクスフォードシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brookeは約1121年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで生まれた。約1148年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアオクスフォードでSir Edmund Brookeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke(은)는 대략 1121년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1148년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 Sir Edmund Brooke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

mn.Oxford, Oxfordshire, England: Oxford, Oxenaford, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke nasceu aproximadamente 1121, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Sir Edmund Brooke aproximadamente 1148, em Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke родилась около 1121 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edmund Brooke около 1148 г. в местности оксфорд, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edward Brooke nació aproximadamente 1121, en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Sir Edmund Brooke aproximadamente 1148, en Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
